lines.content: Bit is a lightweight command-line task manager that helps users organise their tasks quickly and efficiently.
Bit is designed for users who prefer quick command-based task management.
It supports managing todos, deadlines, and events through simple commands, making it ideal for users who prefer fast keyboard-based interaction.
- Add **Deadline** tasks with date and time
- Add **Event** tasks with start and end time
- Mark or unmark tasks as completed
- Tasks are automatically saved and loaded between runs
Prerequisites: **JDK 17** and the latest version of **IntelliJ IDEA**.
If the setup is correct, the program will start in the terminal window.
Tasks are automatically saved to a local data file and will be loaded again when the program starts.
- The `src/main/java` folder must remain the root directory for Java files.
// IMPORTANT: force packaging of runtimeClasspath (includes the OS-specific JavaFX natives)

Bit is a lightweight task manager that helps users manage their tasks efficiently using simple command-line inputs.
Bit allows users to manage tasks quickly using simple command-based inputs.
It supports managing **todos**, **deadlines**, and **events**, and allows users to update, search, and organise tasks quickly.
Below is the graphical interface of Bit.
| `unmark <number>` | Marks a task as not completed |
Adds a simple task to the task list.
The task will be added to the task list.
Adds a task with a deadline.
The task will be added with the specified deadline.
Adds an event with a start and end time.
The event will be added to the task list.
The specified task will be marked as completed.
Marks a task as not completed.
The specified task will be marked as incomplete.
Updates the description of an existing task.
The task description will be updated.
Deletes a task from the list.
The specified task will be removed from the task list.
Searches for tasks containing a keyword.
All tasks containing the keyword will be displayed.
This guide explains how to use Bit to manage tasks efficiently.
* Bit is a simple command-line task manager.
* It supports adding tasks (todo, deadline, event),
* listing tasks, marking/unmarking tasks as done,
* deleting tasks, and exiting the program.
* Tasks are stored using parallel arrays for simplicity.

* JavaFX GUI for the Bit chatbot.

*

This GUI resembles a modern messaging app:

*
  • Date chips ("Today", converted to real date after midnight)

*
  • Welcome sequence from {@link Ui#showWelcome()} displayed every app run

*

The GUI calls {@link Bit#getResponse(String)} to generate replies.
